flags.concern: Video editor lightbox tests: moving `useQuery` into `VideoEditorProvider` will break the existing provider unit test unless the plan also updates the test harness to supply React Query context.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx:1` renders `<VideoEditorProvider>` directly with `@testing-library/react` and no `QueryClientProvider`, while the plan adds `useQuery` inside `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx:1`. In this repo, tests that exercise React Query hooks explicitly wrap with `QueryClientProvider`, e.g. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx:3`. Step 6 only mentions `tsc --noEmit` and a manual smoke test, so it does not cover or repair this existing test surface.
